Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A tale about fixing eBPF spinlock issues in the Linux kernel 87 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. A Decade of Slug 581 upvotes, 53 comments
  3. More than 135 open hardware devices flashable with your own firmware 200 upvotes, 16 comments
  4. Write up of my homebrew CPU build 32 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. JPEG Compression 111 upvotes, 14 comments
  6. Mistral AI Releases Forge 389 upvotes, 73 comments
  7. Unsloth Studio 268 upvotes, 51 comments
  8. Launch HN: Kita (YC W26) – Automate credit review in emerging markets 42 upvotes, 6 comments
  9. Show HN: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes using CoW memory forking 142 upvotes, 36 comments
  10. Review of Microsoft's ClearType Font Collection (2005) 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. Microsoft's 'unhackable' Xbox One has been hacked by 'Bliss' 654 upvotes, 229 comments
  12. Electron microscopy shows ‘mouse bite’ defects in semiconductors 58 upvotes, 14 comments
  13. Why AI systems don't learn – On autonomous learning from cognitive science 103 upvotes, 31 comments
  14. Launch an autonomous AI agent with sandboxed execution in 2 lines of code 33 upvotes, 7 comments
  15. The pleasures of poor product design 94 upvotes, 32 comments
  16. It Took Me 30 Years to Solve This VFX Problem – Green Screen Problem [video] 231 upvotes, 95 comments
  17. Celebrating Tony Hoare's mark on computer science 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  18. I Simulated 38,612 Countryle Games to Find the Best Strategy 20 upvotes, 4 comments
  19. Leviathan (1651) 53 upvotes, 18 comments
  20. Get Shit Done: A meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven dev system 318 upvotes, 153 comments
  21. Python 3.15's JIT is now back on track 362 upvotes, 181 comments
  22. Have a fucking website 332 upvotes, 178 comments
  23. Ryugu asteroid samples contain all DNA and RNA building blocks 234 upvotes, 126 comments
  24. Show HN: Pgit – A Git-like CLI backed by PostgreSQL 20 upvotes, 8 comments
  25. SSH has no Host header 100 upvotes, 79 comments
  26. Aggregated File System (AGFS), a modern tribute to the spirit of Plan 9 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Forget Flags and Scripts: Just Rename the File 30 upvotes, 24 comments
  28. Show HN: The Lottery of Life 18 upvotes, 17 comments
  29. Honda is killing its EVs 312 upvotes, 646 comments
  30. Ndea (YC W26) is hiring a symbolic RL search guidance lead 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 4:30pm, 18 March 2026. Source here.
